howToRun: Each night, but not the first, wake the Architect and ask them to choose a player. Place the [[REDESIGNED]] reminder token next to that player. Place an additional [[REDESIGNED]] reminder token next to a player of the same character type, or choose not to.

If only 1 [[REDESIGNED]] reminder token is on the grimoire, replace the character token of that player with a not-in-play character of the same type. Wake the player and show them the 'You are' card, then their new character token.
If 2 [[REDESIGNED]] reminder tokens are on the grimoire, swap the character tokens of those two players. Wake each player separately and show them the 'You are' card, then their new character token.
examples: Nyla and Bruce are the Actor and Standard Bearer. At night, the Architect chooses Nyla. Nyla and Bruce's character tokens are swapped. They are woken separately and shown their new character token.
Jack is Cleopatra. At night, the Architect chooses Jack. Jack's character token is replaced with the Caesar token. Jack is woken and shown that they are now Caesar.
quote: "I found Rome a city of bricks and left it the city of marbles." — Caesar Augustus 63 B.C.
tags: Fall of Rome, Traveller, Ability, Character Change
otherNightReminder: The Architect points to a player. Choose which ability will effect the chosen player. Treat them accordingly (not-in-play character or character swap). Wake affected players individually. Show 'You are', then their new character token.

name: Sibyl
ability: Each day, after the 1st execution, you may publicly choose a dead player: they may nominate. If the majority of the dead and yourself agree, they are executed.
creator: Alex
lede: The Sibyl consults with the dead, letting them nominate and execute players.
howToRun: After the first execution each day, if the Sibyl publicly chooses a dead player, that dead player may nominate. If the majority of the dead and the Sibyl agree, they are executed.
examples: Murphy dies during the night. Jamie, the good Sibyl travels in. The next day Jamie, after the 1st execution, chooses Murphy. That day Murphy is able to nominate and does so. Talia will receive new information tonight as their 'Sculpture' Murphy nominated a new player.

Sophia is the Glykon. On the first day Sophia was executed and dies. The Sibyl picks Sophia. Sophia nominates a good player, but neither Sophia nor the Sibyl decide to vote on it. The good player is not executed nor are they affected by the Glykon's ability, as Sophia was dead when they nominated.
tips: The majority required is equal to half the number of dead players and the Sibyl. The Sibyl themself does not have to agree if the votes from the dead reach the majority.
Note that the Mortician will gain information from every execution, so is a more powerful role than normal when the Sibyl is in play.
quote: "To such a degree will I be changed that I will be visible to no one; but I will be recognized by my voice. And with my voice, to you, I give a second chance."
tags: Fall of Rome, Traveller, Nomination, Dead

name: High Priest (Pontifex Maximus)
ability: Each day, publicly choose a unique living player to bless: if a majority of players agree, something good happens to them.
creator: Alex
lede: The High Priest can bless players, bringing good fortune to that player and their team.
howToRun: Each day, if the High Priest chooses to bless a player, announce to the town that there will be a vote. Dead players may vote and do not require a ghost vote to do so. If a majority voted place the [[BLESSED]] reminder token next to the chosen player, otherwise place the [[FAILED]] reminder token. That player cannot be selected again by the High Priest. The effect of the blessing is up to the discretion of the storyteller — inherently it should help the player and/or their team.
If the High Priest chooses a player that they have already blessed or tried to bless, ask the High Priest to choose a different player.
examples: Antonio is a Bad Omen who believes they are the Trumpeter. During the day the High Priest chooses to bless Antonio. The town vote and a majority is met. To bless Antonio, the Storyteller removes the BAD OMEN reminder token, so that Antonio will receive true information.

Anne is the Temptress. During the day the High Priest chooses to bless Anne. The town vote and a majority is achieved. After being blessed, Anne's Demon is executed and dies, however the game continues. The Storyteller wakes Anne and shows her the Cleopatra token — Anne is the new Demon.
tips: There are two High Priest variants available. Only one type of High Priest can be in a game of Fall of Rome.

The Pontifex Maximus leads to a more chaotic and random version of Fall of Rome. The Storyteller can freely use the High Priest to provide good or evil players, when blessed, a large range of 'good' things.
The Pontiff provides for a more solvable, information-centric contribution.
quote: "To ignore the signs of the gods is itself to deny their very existence." — Cicero 106 B.C.
tags: Fall of Rome, Traveller, Blessing, Chaos
otherNightReminder: If the High Priest successfully blessed a player today, something good happens to that player and/or their team.

name: High Priest (Pontiff)
ability: Each day, publicly choose a unique living player to bless: if a majority of players agree, tomorrow they may learn a statement. Tonight, choose if it's true.
creator: Alex
lede: The High Priest can bless players, bringing good fortune to that player and their team.
howToRun: Each day, if the High Priest chooses to bless a player, announce to the town that there will be a vote. If a majority voted place the [[BLESSED]] reminder token next to the chosen player, otherwise place the [[FAILED]] reminder token. That player cannot be selected again.

Each night, if the High Priest successfully blessed a player today, wake the High Priest. The High Priest chooses whether the statement is true tomorrow with a hand signal (true 'thumbs up', false 'thumbs down').
tips: There are two High Priest variants available. Only one type of High Priest can be in a game of Fall of Rome.
The Pontiff provides for a more solvable, information-centric contribution, with the added ability for the High Priest to choose whether the blessed players receive true or false information.
quote: "Deny everything, burn the records, say these are lies, anything not to recognise that gods look after our human affairs." — Cicero 106 B.C.
tags: Fall of Rome, Traveller, Blessing, Information
otherNightReminder: If the High Priest successfully blessed a player today, wake the High Priest. The High Priest chooses whether the statement is true tomorrow with a hand signal (true 'thumbs up', false 'thumbs down').

name: Emperor
ability: Each day, choose the 1st execution's outcome. If you choose to protect today's execution: they survive. Otherwise, tonight you learn their alignment.
creator: Alex
lede: The Emperor decides the outcome of the executions — with a thumbs up or a thumbs down.
howToRun: Each day, before the execution goes through ask the Emperor whether they will overturn the execution (thumbs up), or go through with the execution (thumbs down). If they overturn the execution the player executed does not die. The game continues and everyone goes to sleep.

Each night, if the Emperor went through with today's execution (thumbs down), wake the Emperor. They learn the player's alignment. If good, tell them 'good' (thumbs up) otherwise tell them 'evil' (thumbs down).
examples: Patrizia is a Bad Omen who believes they are the Standard Bearer. During the day they are nominated and executed. Before the execution goes through the Storyteller calls upon the Emperor. The Emperor shows a thumbs down. Patrizia is executed and dies. That night, the Emperor learns that Patrizia is evil as they are falsely registering as a Bad Omen.
Gary is Crassus. During the day they are nominated and executed. The Emperor decides to save Gary by showing a thumbs up. Gary is executed and does not die, and the game continues.
quote: "Which death is preferable to every other? The unexpected."
tags: Fall of Rome, Traveller, Execution, Information
otherNightReminder: If a player was executed today and the outcome was not overturned by the Emperor: Show the hand signal (thumbs down 'evil', thumbs up 'good') for the players alignment.

name: "I Am Spartacus!"
ability: Each day, up to half the living players (rounded up & Travellers don't count) may publicly claim to be Spartacus.
creator: Alex
lede: Living players may publicly stake their claim as Spartacus each day.
howToRun: At the start of the day, remove all [[I AM SPARTACUS!]] reminder tokens, if any.
During the day, if a player publicly claims to be Spartacus place the [[I AM SPARTACUS!]] reminder token next to that player.
Only up to half the living players (rounded up and not counting Travellers) may publicly claim to be Spartacus.
Players may ask the Storyteller during the day who has publicly claimed to be Spartacus so far. This is public information that the Storyteller can divulge.
examples: Mack is Spartacus. During the day, Mack publicly claims to be Spartacus. The Storyteller places the I AM SPARTACUS! reminder token next to Spartacus. Spartacus' ability condition is met, and they might register as a Townsfolk.

Gary is Crassus. During the day, Gary publicly claims to be Spartacus. The Storyteller places the I AM SPARTACUS! reminder token next to Crassus. Crassus' ability condition is met — if they die today or tonight, a living evil player becomes Crassus.
tips: Mechanical Spartacus claims are a large part of the game. The Storyteller should emphasise this at the start of the game, and reiterate that they are happy to state who has claimed Spartacus each day when asked. The Storyteller should also inform players how many mechanical claims are left.
Consider using physical markers visible in the Town Square to show who has claimed Spartacus. Flags and hats are a fun way to show who has claimed Spartacus today.
Make clear to the players that you will not be able to confirm Spartacus claims from previous days, only the current day.
quote: "The ego cuts deeper than the flesh." — Spartacus c. 103 B.C.

name: Bard
ability: Each day, publicly choose any living players: recently chosen Townsfolk are drunk. Each night*, learn how many are drunk this way.
creator: Taiyi
lede: The Bard can get Townsfolk drunk to learn their numbers.
summaryBullets: The Bard learns the number of "Drunk" tokens placed at the moment the information is revealed to them.
The drunkenness caused by the Bard lasts from the moment they publicly select a player until the next Bard's public selection.

Aim to have the Bard make only one public selection per day; repeatedly adjusting drunk tokens is cumbersome. The Bard cannot retract a player they have already selected, but if necessary, they may supplement their selection with additional players.
If a Townsfolk drunkened by the Bard becomes a non-townsfolk role, that player immediately regains sobriety (drunkenness is removed).
The Bard may only select living players during their choice. If a Townsfolk they select dies during the night, it does not affect the Bard's determination that they are drunk.
howToRun: At any point during the day, the Bard may publicly select any living player. After selection, place a [[DRUNK]] token next to the chosen townsfolk's token and remove any previously placed [[DRUNK]] tokens.
These players become drunk from this point onward until the Bard makes another public selection.
During the night phase, wake the Bard and show them the number of "Drunk" tokens currently in play.
examples: On the first day, the Bard publicly chooses three players, including two Townsfolk. The two Townsfolk players immediately become drunk. That night, the Bard wakes and learns "2".

On the first day, the Bard publicly chooses three players, including two Townsfolk. The Townsfolk immediately become drunk. That night, the Demon kills the Bard. The 2 Townsfolk become sober and their abilities work normally that night.
On the first day, the Bard chooses the Gambler, who then becomes drunk. That night the Pit-Hag turns the Gambler into the Klutz. As the Klutz is no longer a Townsfolk, they become sober.
tips: Before publicly declaring your choice, it's best to communicate with the player you intend to select to avoid drunkening crucial Townsfolk roles. Of course, you may choose not to do so, but you risk being executed outright.
If you wish to select a player with a daytime ability like the Artist, it's best to let them activate their ability first. Otherwise, your selection may cause them to become drunk immediately.
Encourage other players to claim they are the Bard. This makes it difficult for the Demon to identify the real Bard among you early on.
You may choose only Townsfolk who have already used their abilities. This both verifies their Townsfolk status and avoids drunkening crucial Townsfolk.
You can choose only Outsiders. If you learn they are Townsfolk, your information is definitely wrong.

Before selecting too many players, think carefully. Getting a large number of Townsfolk drunk might not be a good idea for the good team, and it's hard to tell who's a townsfolk and who isn't in a crowd of players.
quote: Souls touched by my fingertips shall plunge into dreams. While you stagger in alcohol's embrace, I count the vibrating silk strings... Three? Five? These numbers reveal how many honest souls expose lies' shapes within sweet slumber.
otherNightReminder: Wake the Bard. Show them the number of DRUNK tokens currently in play.

name: Archlich
ability: Each night*, choose a player: they die. The 1st time you choose each dead player, they choose 2 players: chosen non-demons die.
creator: Taiyi
lede: The Archlich can infuse corpses with energy, granting them tremendous power. The Archlich may give up their own kill, allowing a corpse to act in their place — killing 2 players that night. However, the corpse controls who dies, and each corpse can only be used once.
howToRun: Each night except the first, wake the Archlich. Have the Archlich point to any player. Put the Archlich to sleep.
If they chose a living player, mark that player with the [[DEAD]] reminder token.

If they chose a dead player who does not already have a [[TRIGGERED]] reminder token: wake that dead player and show them in sequence the THIS ABILITY AFFECTS YOU reminder token and the Archlich character token. Place the [[TRIGGERED]] reminder token next to that player's character token. Have them choose 2 players. Mark whichever of those 2 players is not the Demon with the [[DEAD]] reminder token.
At dawn, announce which players died during the night.
Choosing a dead Traveller does not trigger the Archlich's ability. If the Archlich chooses a dead Traveller, inform them that this does not activate their ability.
callout: Each player can trigger the Archlich's ability at most once. The [[TRIGGERED]] token is never removed for any reason. All Archliches and players with the Archlich's ability share a single pool of already-triggered dead players — even if the original Archlich dies or is replaced.
examples: On the second night, the Archlich chooses the living Poisoner. The Poisoner dies. The following night, the Archlich chooses the dead Poisoner. The Poisoner chooses the Noble and the Fortune Teller. The Noble and the Fortune Teller die.

On the second night, the Archlich chooses the dead Poisoner. The Poisoner chooses the Noble and the Fortune Teller. They die. The following day, the Poisoner publicly nominates the Hell's Castaway. The Hell's Castaway and the Archlich swap characters, becoming the new Archlich. That night, the new Archlich chooses the dead Poisoner — nobody dies. Each player can only trigger the Archlich's ability once, regardless of which Archlich does the choosing.

On the first day, the Noble dies by execution. That night, the Archlich chooses the dead Noble. The Noble chooses the Poisoner and the Archlich. The Poisoner dies. At dawn, the Noble immediately announces they were chosen by the Archlich.

On the first day, the Noble dies by execution. That night, the Archlich chooses the dead Noble. The Noble chooses 2 dead players. Nobody dies. The following night, the Archlich chooses the dead Noble again — the Noble is not woken, as each player can only trigger the ability once.
tips: As the Archlich, you must learn to make use of corpses. If you only ever choose living players, you are playing as a perfectly ordinary Demon with no special ability. Every dead player is a resource — think carefully about how to use the dead to your advantage.
Choosing a dead evil player is a smart move. They will kill in your interests and will not voluntarily reveal your existence — but the number of dead evil players is always limited.

Dead good players are an inexhaustible supply. However, the good team will learn you exist when a dead good player announces they were chosen. They may also choose targets you'd rather they didn't — including you. If they choose you and you don't die, they will be almost certain you are the Demon.
quote: I heard you. The resentment in your soul toward death, the hunger for power. The world fears you — and I admire you. Accept this gift. Your corpse will become my first masterpiece, and your will shall become the law by which all living things are judged. Breathe deep this sweet terror... it will be the first meal of your new life.
otherNightReminder: The Archlich points to a player. If living: that player dies. If dead and no TRIGGERED token: wake that dead player, show THIS ABILITY AFFECTS YOU and the Archlich token, place TRIGGERED. They choose 2 players — whichever is not the Demon dies.
fighting: If multiple deaths seem Archlich-caused but no player has claimed to have been chosen, at least one previously dead player must be evil — most likely one who died by execution.
If a player claims they were chosen by the Archlich and the living player they chose did not die, that player who didn't die is almost certainly the Demon.

Do not blindly trust dead players who claim they were chosen by the Archlich. Evil players can use this tactic to steer the good team into incorrect assumptions or lead the town to execute a good player.

If you are a dead good player woken by the Archlich, choose carefully. You could pick 2 dead players to minimise further deaths, or choose players you believe to be evil to reduce the evil team's numbers. The choice is yours.
If you were previously woken by the Archlich and the living player you chose did not die, that player is almost certainly the Demon. Announce this publicly.
